codeReview.summary: The responsive-design feature was a verification-only task. The worker correctly determined that no code changes were needed because the existing Tailwind CSS responsive utility classes already provide correct responsive behavior across all target viewports (320px, 375px, 768px, 1440px). The worker verified this with Playwright CDP-based testing at all four viewport sizes plus a full mobile game flow test and a resize reflow test. All verification passed. I independently reviewed the responsive classes in all components and confirm they satisfy all requirements.

issues.description: Square text is 11px on mobile (`text-[11px]`) which is at the lower boundary of readability. While the worker verified readability via browser automation and it meets the stated requirement ('min 11-12px'), 11px text in 54px-wide cells with long bingo item labels will wrap heavily. Consider bumping to 12px for improved readability. This is a polish concern, not a functional bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The responsive design breakpoint strategy is implicit across components but not documented. The project uses Tailwind's default `sm:` breakpoint (640px) as the sole responsive breakpoint for the board. The pattern is: mobile-first defaults + `sm:` variants for tablet/desktop. This works well given the binary nature of the layout (mobile vs. not-mobile), but it's not documented anywhere in AGENTS.md or architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Square.tsx uses `text-[11px] sm:text-xs`, `min-h-[4.5rem] sm:min-h-20`, `px-1.5 sm:px-2`; Board.tsx uses `gap-1 sm:gap-2`; game.tsx uses `text-3xl sm:text-4xl`; index.tsx uses `text-5xl sm:text-7xl`. All use the same sm: (640px) breakpoint pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker SKILL.md says 'Write Tests First (Red)' as Step 2, but for this verification-only feature where no code changes were needed, the worker correctly skipped test writing. The skill procedure doesn't account for verification-only scenarios where existing code already meets requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff confirms skillFeedback.followedProcedure = true with no deviations. Worker ran existing test suite (161/161 passing) and used Playwright browser automation for manual verification instead of writing new tests.

summary: PASS — The responsive-design feature is verification-only; no code changes were needed. The existing Tailwind responsive utility classes (grid-cols-5, sm: breakpoint variants, max-w-lg container, min-h-[4.5rem] touch targets, viewport meta tag) already satisfy all requirements across 320px, 375px, 768px, and 1440px viewports. The worker thoroughly verified with Playwright CDP testing at all viewport sizes, including a full mobile game flow and resize reflow test. One non-blocking note: 11px text on 320px viewports is at the lower boundary of readability for long bingo item labels.
